# hrobot_ssh_key (Resource)

Manages an SSH key in the Hetzner Robot account. The key can be referenced when activating rescue or Linux installation boot configs.

## Example Usage

```terraform
resource "hrobot_ssh_key" "example" {
  name       = "deploy"
  public_key = "ssh-ed25519 AAAAC3NzaC1lZDI1NTE5AAAAIExampleKeyMaterial deploy@example"
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `name` (String) Key name. Can be changed in place.
- `public_key` (String) The public key material (e.g. `ssh-ed25519 AAAA...`). Changing it replaces the key.

### Read-Only

- `created_at` (String) Creation timestamp (RFC 3339).
- `fingerprint` (String) Fingerprint assigned by Hetzner; the identifier used for import.
- `id` (String) Key fingerprint (mirrors `fingerprint`).
- `size` (Number) Key size in bits.
- `type` (String) Key algorithm (e.g. ED25519, RSA).

## Import

Import is supported using the following syntax:

The [`terraform import` command](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/cli/commands/import) can be used, for example:

```shell
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# SSH keys are imported by their fingerprint.
terraform import hrobot_ssh_key.example 56:29:99:a4:5d:ed:ac:d7:6d:ed:ac:d7:6d:ed:ac:d7
```
